item.description: The army in junta-ruled Mali on Saturday battled what it called ‘terrorist groups’ that launched surprise attacks around the capital Bamako and other parts of the west African nation......

item.description: Hopes for progress in the second phase of Iran–US talks have collapsed after Iran’s foreign minister left Pakistan without engaging in negotiations, while US president Donald Trump cancelled a planned visit by his envoys to Islamabad....

item.title: Turkey fires deputy minister after school shootings
item.description: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has dismissed Turkey’s deputy education minister after two school shootings that left nine people dead, according to the official gazette published late Friday......

item.title: Wikimedia, Indonesia reach blackout deal
item.description: The Wikimedia Foundation said Saturday it has found a compromise with the Indonesian government, which had threatened to block Wikipedia over domestic registration rules the foundation feared were overly invasive......

item.title: Sunamganj farmers asked to harvest boro by April 28
item.description: The Bangladesh Water Development Board has asked the farmers to harvest their 80-per cent-ripe boro paddy and take it home quickly by April 28, due to fears of heavy rainfall and flash floods in Sunamganj within the next few days....

item.description: Indian police said Saturday that 47 Maoist rebels have surrendered, nearly a month after the country was declared free of the decades-long insurgency......

item.description: The police strengthened their surveillance and security over threats of attacks by banned extremist organisations on important state establishments, places of religious worship, recreation centres and the capital city’s Shahbagh crossing....

item.title: Single-screen cinemas on way to extinction
item.description: The steady decline of single-screen cinemas in the capital and elsewhere across the country marks a significant shift in the access of people to entertainment, though moviegoing has historically been a primary means of mass entertainment....

item.description: Former New Zealand and Essex all-rounder Doug Bracewell has been given a two-year suspension after testing positive for cocaine during an English County Championship match last year......

item.description: Bangladesh’s women’s national cricket team suffered a seven-wicket defeat to their Sri Lankan counterparts in the third and final Women’s one-day international to lose the series 2-1 at the Rajshahi Divisional Stadium on Saturday......

item.description: DURING some of the worst days of the illegal US-Israeli war on Iran, I was talking to friends who were in the civilian areas being bombed......

item.title: Ensuring fertiliser security
item.description: RECENT tension involving the United States, Israel and Iran in the Middle East once again drew global attention to the vulnerability of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most strategic maritime corridors. Although a ceasefire has eased immediate fears of disruption, the episode has underscored how fragile this passage remains and how quickly instability in the region can ripple across global supply chains......

item.title: Fight for nutritional fairness
item.description: BANGLADESH is frequently praised for its impressive strides in reducing child undernutrition over the past two decades, and these national gains deserve recognition. Yet the broad averages often conceal a more difficult truth: progress has not reached everyone equally. As National Nutrition Week 2026 draws attention to the state of public health, it is necessary to......Bangladesh travel guide

item.title: Impact on maternal, neonatal health
item.description: BANGLADESH stands on the frontline of climate change. Rising temperatures, erratic rainfall, cyclones, floods and creeping salinity are no longer distant environmental concerns. They are daily realities. While much of the national conversation focuses on infrastructure damage, crop loss or economic costs, a quieter and more devastating crisis is unfolding: the growing......

item.title: Large-scale Dhaka transport projects behind promises
item.description: THE long-promised development of public transport for Dhaka with projects meant to ease congestion and reduce hassles remains disappointingly slow. A series of large-scale projects, including mass rapid transit lines, a bus rapid transit corridor and an elevated expressway, have advanced unevenly, with visible gaps between planning and execution. In 2014, the first-ever......

item.title: Multi-sector approach needed to curb hunger
item.description: THE Global Report on Food Crises 2026, which the Global Network against Food Crises released on April 24, has placed Bangladesh among 10 countries that are home to two-thirds of the world’s people who face high levels of acute hunger. The other nine countries are Afghanistan, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Myanmar, Nigeria, Pakistan, South Sudan, Sudan, the......Newspapers

item.description: The National Board of Revenue has clarified that there is no plan to introduce an inheritance tax in the upcoming 2026–27 national budget, while outlining broader efforts to expand the country’s tax base by bringing more informal economic activities under the tax net......
